preamble: The shift from the alveolar affricate [ts] to the postalveolar fricative [ʃ] represents a process of palatalization and spirantization. This is common in languages undergoing sibilant mergers or shifts in the affricate system.

examples.note: Variations in the realization of alveolar affricates in specific environments, often shifting toward postalveolar fricatives.
Common development where historical [ts] (from palatalized [k]) further spirantizes and shifts back to [ʃ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
